ai.disclaimer: ⚠️ Disclaimer: This is an AI assistant. Always consult a real zootechnist or veterinarian before applying any decisions.
ai.contextInfo: 🧠 The AI assistant can access the following data in this chat: your last 5 saved rations (including ingredient amounts and full nutrient analysis), saved animal profiles, last 5 field observations, and your current active ration session.
ai.systemPrompt:
You are a Senior Chief Consultant with the highest level of academic and practical knowledge in Agricultural Engineering, Zootechny, and Veterinary Medicine, fully proficient in NASEM 2021, NRC 2001, and CNCPS standards. Your expertise is not limited to dairy cattle nutrition; it covers all agricultural and zootechnical disciplines, including Zoology, Animal Production Systems, Animal Ecology, Agricultural Biotechnology, Agricultural Mechanization, Forage Crops and Pasture Management, Entomology and Phytopathology (partially), Animal Nutrition, Nutritional Ecology, Statistics, Animal Anatomy and Physiology, Animal Housing, Nutritional Biochemistry, Feed Science, Additives and Toxicology, Ethology, Genetics, Breeding, Animal Health, Hygiene and Diseases, Reproduction and Artificial Insemination, Production Physiology, Ruminant and Poultry Nutrition, Cattle and Sheep Breeding, Health Protection Management, Herd Management, and Economic Analysis.

The user's farm data is provided in the following JSON structure:

{{data}}

This JSON contains the following sections:

**currentSession** — The currently active ration session:
  - animal: animal profile (bw, milkYield, milkFat, milkProtein, breed, dim, parity)
  - feeds: selected feeds and amounts
  - rationResult: optimization result (dmi, nel, cp, ndf, cost, statusName)

**rationHistory** — The user's last 5 saved ration snapshots:
  - name, savedAt, status, totalCost
  - animal: animal profile for that ration
  - ingredients: list of ration components. For each ingredient: name (feed name), dmKg (DM kg/day), asFedKg (as-fed kg/day), pctDm (% of DM), costPerDay (daily cost)
  - composition: full nutrient analysis. Fields: nel_mcal (NEL Mcal/day), cp_g (CP g/day), cp_pct (CP % DM), rup_g (RUP g/day), rdp_g (RDP g/day), ndf_pct (NDF % DM), adf_pct (ADF % DM), nfc_pct (NFC % DM), starch_pct (starch % DM), fat_pct (fat % DM), ash_pct (ash % DM), ca_g (Ca g/day), p_g (P g/day), mg_g (Mg g/day), k_g (K g/day), na_g (Na g/day), dcad_meq (DCAD mEq/100g)
  - requirements: target NEL and MP values
  - milkFever: milk fever risk score

**animalProfiles** — All saved animal profiles (name, breed, bw, milkYield, parity, lactationStage, dim)

**recentObservations** — Last 5 field observations (date, milkYield, milkFat, milkProtein, bcs, dmiActual, notes)

**farm** — Farm and herd information (name, address, advisor, herdSize)

Your tasks:
1. Answer the user's question using your extensive agricultural, zootechnical, and veterinary knowledge, taking the provided farm data into account.
2. When comparing rations, actively use the ingredients and composition fields; report ingredient amounts and nutrient values concretely.
3. Interpret field observation trends (how have milk yield, BCS, and DMI changed over time?).
4. If data is missing or null, inform the user; if you cannot produce a result, explain why.
5. Present practical, actionable, and scientific advice in a balanced way; include academic references when needed.

VERY IMPORTANT RULES:
1. STRICTLY AND ONLY RESPOND IN ENGLISH. Never use Chinese, Japanese, or any other languages! Your response must be 100% flawless English.
2. ALWAYS format your responses in Markdown. Use **bold** for emphasis, bullet points for lists, and create tables when necessary.
3. Your response must be highly professional, clear, and strike an academic/practical balance.
4. Even for short questions, provide sufficiently comprehensive and contextual answers; actively use the user's data.
5. If the user asks a completely off-topic question unrelated to agriculture, farming, zootechny, or veterinary medicine (e.g., music, politics), do not throw an error. Instead, politely reply: 'I am a specialized assistant in agriculture, zootechny, and veterinary medicine, and I cannot assist with topics outside this scope.'

dashboard.no_reminders: No pending alerts. Everything looks good.
dashboard.herd_ready: {n} profiles ready for farm panel calculation
dashboard.iofc_no_herd: Add a herd group first (animal count).
dashboard.iofc_note: ⓘ Estimate: current ration × total animal count. Use the Farm Panel tab for different periods.
dashboard.no_obs_7d: No observations in the last 7 days.
dashboard.need_2_obs: At least 2 measurements needed for the trend chart (1 record in the last 7 days).
dashboard.rem_relaxed_x: {n} constraints not met; review the results.
dashboard.rem_infeasible_x: The LP optimizer found no solution. Review feed selection or constraints.
dashboard.rem_my_down_x: Slope {slope} kg/measurement — check ration or health.
dashboard.rem_obs_late_x: {d} days since the last record. Weekly measurement recommended.
dashboard.rem_no_obs_x: Start performance tracking by recording your first observations.

animal.stage_hint: Stage selection auto-adjusts DCAD and CP targets
animal.breed_hint: Urine pH interpretation varies by breed (Holstein/Jersey distinction)
animal.parity_hint: Parity = number of calvings. For a heifer (never calved) select a dry stage.
animal.adg_hint: Primiparous cows keep growing → extra NEL/MP (NRC 2001 Ch. 11). Leave empty to skip growth.
animal.fetch_weather_title: Fetches current temperature and humidity using the location in Settings
animal.thi_hint: THI is computed automatically when temperature + humidity are both entered (Mader 2006). 72+ heat stress, 78+ severe.
animal.daily_walk_hint: e.g., pasture or parlor distance. Leave 0 for confined systems.
animal.title: Animal Profile

ration.feed_info: You can add feeds from the Feed Library tab or quickly add them below.
ration.no_feeds: No feed selected yet. Add from the library or above.
ration.type_help: How the feed is handled in the LP — Continuous: any value (e.g. 4.37 kg). Min order: the feed is either 0 or at least 'Min kg' (a meaningful dose for premixes/additives; Min kg must be entered). Integer: the amount must be whole kg (bag/lot-based purchasing).
ration.milp_minorder_warn: For 'Min order', enter Min kg (preferably Max kg too) for this feed — otherwise it behaves as continuous.
ration.drag_hint: Drag the ⠿ handle to reorder feeds.
ration.drag_to_add: Drag onto the list to add to ration (or click)
ration.dynamic_targets: Dynamic targets: Composition constraints calibrated for milk yield ({milk} kg), parity ({parity}), BCS ({bcs}) {thi}.
ration.anionic_warning: ⚠️ Anionic ration: DCAD must be kept negative (milk fever prevention).
ration.nelmp_info: If NEL/MP are left empty, the calculated animal requirements (placeholder) are used. Enter values to override (e.g. raise NEL min for high target yield).
ration.advanced_constraints: Advanced Constraints (Starch / Sugar / Fat / RDP + Trace / Vitamins)
ration.advanced_info: These constraints are applied automatically per the science system; enter values to override. Trace/vitamin limits are enforced only if a source feed (or premix) provides them.
ration.legend_computed: Faint blue italic value = computed requirement — enforced as a constraint even if left blank
ration.legend_optional: Faint gray hint (min/max/example) = optional — applied only if you type a value
ration.computed_default_hint: Computed requirement — applied as a constraint even if you leave this blank
ration.eaa_optional_note: Other EAAs (Arg/Thr/Ile/Leu/Val/Phe/Trp) — OPTIONAL: become an LP constraint only if you enter a value (no default constraint; rarely limiting). The gray number is a reference only; achieved values are in Results > Amino Acids.
ration.relax_priority_info: If the ration is infeasible, constraints are relaxed in this order: the BOTTOM ones relax FIRST, the TOP ones are protected longest. DMI/NEL/MP and macro minerals are always protected (not in the list). Reorder with the arrow buttons.
ration.tmr_moisture_note: Recommended 45-55% moisture. Setting a target steers the LP toward wet feeds (silage) → part of the water comes from the ration; the upper bound prevents excess silage. Leave empty to disable.
ration.tmr_moisture_info2: Set the target TMR moisture and the minimum share of it that must come from ration ingredients; the rest is made up with a calculated amount of added WATER. More flexible than a flat moisture band in the dry period.
ration.tmr_target_note: Total moisture % the mixed ration (including added water) should reach (e.g. 50). Leave empty to hide the water calc/chart.
ration.tmr_min_ration_note: Minimum share (percentage points) of the target moisture that must come from the ration's own water. E.g. target 50, min 30 → ration ≥30%, remaining 20% from added water. Steers the ration toward wet feeds; the added-water amount is computed in Results.
ration.hard_info: Checked constraints are 'required': they are never relaxed (violated), even if no feasible ration exists. DMI, NEL (energy) and MP (protein) are always required. Making a constraint required may yield 'no feasible ration' if it cannot be met.
ration.dmi_tolerance_hint: Allowed deviation from the dry-matter intake target (default 3%). Lower = strict (closer to optimum), higher = easier feasibility.
ration.carb_fiber: Carbohydrate & Fiber (NDF / ADF / NFC / peNDF / Forage)
ration.group_limits_info: Limit total inclusion per category. E.g. "Protein Sources max 8 kg" or "Roughages min 12 kg". Empty fields are not applied.
ration.cost_max_hint: Daily feed cost cannot exceed this value (budget constraint).
ration.multi_obj_info: If you assign weights (≥1), a multi-objective balance is used instead of a single objective. Cost + milk fat depression (MFD) risk + amino acid adequacy are optimized together. Scale-independent: weights are pure priority (0 = ignore, 5 = highest).
ration.obj_mutex: Multi-objective active — this single objective is ignored
ration.select_feed_warn: Please select at least one feed.
ration.profile_missing_warn: Animal profile is incomplete. Please fill in the Animal Profile tab first.

results.relaxed_box:
⚠️ Strict constraints could not be met — constraints were relaxed in priority order.
The ration below was computed with these relaxations (you still get a suggestion). The following targets could not be met:
results.relaxed_hint: Tip: add the relevant feed/premix or manually relax the top (most critical) constraint.
results.infeasible_hint: Relax constraints or add more/different feeds. Especially check forage (40-70%), NDF (28-38%) and mineral balance.
results.iis_hint: These constraints cannot be met simultaneously — relaxing at least one makes the ration feasible.
results.sum_asfed_title: Total as-fed (wet) ration weight — TMR mix amount (incl. water)
results.tmr_water_title: TMR Moisture Balance & Water to Add
results.tmr_water_note: Add {kg} kg/day water to the TMR to reach {t}% moisture (total as-fed ≈ {mass} kg/day).
results.tmr_already_wet: Ration is already wetter than target ({m}% moisture) → no added water needed.
results.mp_title: MP — Metabolizable Protein (LP-determining target, NDS/AMTS standard)
results.cp_title: CP — Crude Protein (informational; not an LP constraint, MP is determining)
results.card_rumen_ph: 📈 Rumen pH Prediction Chart (Educational - Not Validated)
results.card_fatty_acid: 🥛 Fatty Acid Profile & Milk Fat Estimate
results.display_badge: 📊 Display & Analysis — backed by CNCPS iterative formulation
results.advisory_added: {n} additive(s) added to the feed list — re-optimize from Ration Builder.
results.advisory_exists: This additive is already in the feed list.
results.dmi_fill_note: 🛑 DMI fill adjustment: High NDF ({ndf}%) fills the rumen, so estimated intake was reduced {base} → {adj} kg/day (Mertens fill limit). Energy/protein targets are evaluated at this lower DMI — if there's a deficit, lower ration NDF / improve forage quality.
results.energy_discount_note: ⚡ Intake-level energy discount ({pct}%): At high intake (multiples of maintenance) the feed's effective TDN/NEL drops (NRC 2001 — faster passage rate). The energy target is met at this discounted value. Can be turned off in Settings.
results.dmi_fill_card_title: Animal-based DMI {base} kg; reduced due to ration NDF fill
results.cncps_engine_note: CNCPS iterative engine: ration converged in {iter} iteration(s). Passage rates (%/h): liquid {liquid}, concentrate {conc}, forage {rough}. Protein degradation (RDP/RUP) computed from passage rate — drives formulation.
results.cncps_engine_unconv: CNCPS iterative engine: did not fully converge in {iter} iterations; last (best) solution used. Results still reflect passage-dependent protein degradation.
results.cncps_engine_fallback: CNCPS calculation mode: no initial solution found, fell back to NRC single-pass. Review the feed set/constraints.
results.missing_sources: No source in feed set — premix/supplement required
results.go_optimize: Go to Ration Builder to optimize

econ.dry_herd_hint: Number of animals in the group
econ.dry_info: Info: Milk Revenue and IOFC are not calculated for non-lactating (Dry/Transition) animals. Only daily ration cost is shown.
econ.row_raw_eff_note: NRC 2001 conventional calc — ECM is the modern standard
econ.iofc_loss_m: Ration cost exceeds milk revenue. Review feed prices or increase yield.
econ.iofc_excellent_m: Your feed cost is very low or your milk price is very high.
econ.iofc_good_m: IOFC above the industry average, sustainable profitability.
econ.iofc_medium_m: IOFC is acceptable, but there is room for improvement.
econ.iofc_low_m: IOFC is low; tightly control feed costs and optimize the ration.
econ.eff_low_m: Improvement needed — may be fiber/peNDF deficiency

aa.eaa_display_note: Arg/Thr/Ile/Leu/Val/Phe/Trp are for DISPLAY: supply is largely from microbial protein; feed values for the major types (grains/protein/forage, ~42% of feeds) come from NRC 2001 Table 15-1 type profiles, with typical RUP assumed for the remaining byproducts. These AAs are rarely limiting in dairy cows — formulation is driven by Lys/Met/His (LP constraints only on those).
aa.his_note: Most His supply comes from microbial protein; feed-based His is measured only in the major protein sources (category-estimated for others). His is the critical 3rd-limiting AA in dairy cows (especially grass/corn-silage-based, low-bypass rations).
aa.supply_note: Lys supply: {lysMcp}g microbial + {lysRup}g RUP. Met supply: {metMcp}g microbial + {metRup}g RUP. RUP profile: Lys {lysPct}%, Met {metPct}%.

dcad.est_note: DCAD-based approximate estimate. Field validation recommended. Target: {range}.
dcad.footer_measured: Field-measured pH = most reliable indicator. Take a morning urine sample 2-3 days before calving and measure it fresh.
dcad.footer_estimated: Urine pH: Should be validated with a field pH meter. If the "Urine pH" field in the animal profile is filled in, that value takes priority.
dcad.msg_optimal: DCAD is in the optimal range ({label}: {min} to {max} mEq/100g DM)
dcad.msg_below_target: DCAD is below target. For {label}, a minimum of {min} mEq/100g DM is recommended.
dcad.msg_above_target: DCAD is above target. For {label}, a maximum of {max} mEq/100g DM is recommended.
dcad.phmsg_normal: Urine pH is in the normal range for this period ({label}: {min}-{max}).
dcad.phmsg_unexpected_acidic: Unexpectedly low pH for this period. Anion load or dietary S may be excessive.
dcad.phmsg_normal_high: High pH is normal for this period.
dcad.phmsg_target_met: TARGET: pH in the {min}-{max} range. Acidogenesis is sufficient, Ca absorption is optimized.
dcad.phmsg_insufficient_acidification: Insufficient acidogenesis. Increase the anionic salt dose by 10-20%, make DCAD more negative.
dcad.phmsg_no_acidification: No acidogenesis — high milk fever risk. Verify the anionic salt protocol, measure DCAD.
dcad.phmsg_borderline_low: Borderline low. Continue monitoring; observe the animal's appetite and behavior.
dcad.phmsg_over_acidification: Over-acidosis — risk of DMI suppression and metabolic acidosis. Reduce the anionic salt dose by 15-25%.
dcad.anionic_msg: To bring DCAD down to the target ({target} mEq/100g), a deficit of {meq} mEq of anions per day must be covered.

rumen.safe_box: ✓ Rumen pH profile is in the safe range. SARA risk is low.
rumen.dyn_footer:
Model: Allen (1997) + Krause & Oetzel (2006) + Plaizier (2008). Hourly pH values are an approximate estimate derived from NFC/peNDF/starch/sugar/fat and forage ratio (the simulation model has not been academically validated). Computed over 2 main meals/day (morning/evening).
Validation: Chart data has not been validated on the animal (educational/predictive only). For definitive results, use a bolus pH meter or rumen-cannulated devices.
rumen.health_ok_box: ✓ Rumen health parameters are in appropriate ranges.
rumen.health_footer:
SARA (Subacute Rumen Acidosis): rumen pH dropping below 5.5 daily. Associated with milk fat depression, undigested grain in feces, lameness and laminitis.
This score is an approximate estimate based on ration composition; it should be validated with actual rumen pH measurement.

fa.denovo: De novo Milk Fat Synthesis Suppression
fa.cla_note: cis-9, trans-11 CLA — functional nutrient (anti-cancer, immune)
fa.footer:
Model: Glasser et al. (2008) JDS + Chilliard et al. (2007) + Palmquist (2009). The fatty acid profile is estimated from typical feed category values; if the faProfile field is provided in the feed analysis, it takes priority.
n-6/n-3 ratio: a milk quality indicator for human nutrition; ideal ≤4, max ≤10. Flaxseed, pasture forage and fish meal enrich n-3.

vm.func_bcarotene: Fertility, cystic ovary prevention, vit A precursor
vm.premix_info: 💡 Recommendation: Use a premix supplement for vitamin/trace mineral deficits. A typical lactation premix at 500-1000 g/day provides {a} IU vit A, {d} IU vit D, {e} IU vit E, and all trace minerals.
vm.tox_info: ⛔ Toxicity Warning: One or more trace minerals exceed the upper limit — reduce the premix dose. Se (max 2 mg/kg DM) and Cu (max 40 mg/kg DM) have especially high toxicity risk.
vm.bgroup_intro: 🧫 B Group (advisory — premix, not in LP): The rumen normally synthesizes enough B-vitamins; supplementation only matters in transition/high-yield/stress. For this period/yield, rumen-protected
vm.bgroup_outro: combination may increase milk protein yield (Girard & Matte 2005). B6/B2 rumen synthesis is usually sufficient.

settings.description: Manage application preferences, unit systems, and your farm details here.
settings.requirement_hint: Determines maintenance energy/protein, BCS mobilization and mineral coefficients.
settings.dmi_hint: Dry matter intake prediction equation. 'Auto': NASEM/INRA → de Souza 2019, NRC 2001 → NRC 2001 (can be temporarily changed in Ration Builder).
settings.dmi_migrated_toast: Your DMI (dry matter intake) method setting was updated to 'Auto' for consistency with the science system (NASEM/INRA → de Souza 2019). You can change it in Settings.
settings.energy_discount_hint: NRC 2001: at high intake (multiples of maintenance) feed TDN/NEL digestibility drops (~4%/multiple). When on, energy is discounted ~6-8% for high-yield cows (realistic energy gap becomes visible); ~0 for dry/maintenance cows. Recommended (on).
settings.calc_mode_hint: Default is NRC single-pass. CNCPS mode iteratively computes passage rate (from intake/concentrate ratio). For measured feeds, it adjusts protein degradation and Energy MCP (fermentable-CHO based) accordingly (≤5 iterations). Opt-in (experimental).
settings.farm_profile_hint: Default/fallback profile for new farms. For multi-farm use, each farm's own name/address is set from the top-menu farm editor and appears in that farm's PDF reports.
settings.location_hint: This location is used when fetching weather data.
settings.default_hint: Pre-fill values when creating a new animal profile and ration.
settings.units_hint: Applied to main indicators (body weight, temperature). The calculation core is always metric.
settings.theme_hint: Can also be changed via the theme button in the top bar or Alt+T.
settings.data_hint: All animal profiles, herd groups, rations, price history, field observations and your settings are backed up to a single JSON file. Use it when changing devices or for data safety.
settings.clear_cache_hint: Clears cached app files and refreshes (your data is not deleted)
settings.clear_cache_confirm: Cached app files will be cleared and the page reloaded. Your data (profiles/rations) is NOT deleted. Continue?
settings.data_warning: ⚠️ Restore and Clear overwrite existing data — backing up first is recommended.
settings.title: Settings

heat.adj_dmi: ✓ DMI reduced (West 2003: ~0.4 kg per unit above THI>72)
heat.no_stress_box: ✓ THI {v} — no heat stress adjustment needed (threshold: 72).

env.msg_low: Emission per unit milk is low — indicates high nutritional efficiency.
env.msg_normal: Typical range for dairy cattle (~12–18 g CH₄/kg milk).
env.msg_high: Emission per unit milk is high — yield and/or ration efficiency can be improved.
env.msg_very_high: Emission per unit milk is very high — ration and herd efficiency should be reviewed.
env.msg_na: No milk production, so intensity is not calculated; daily CH₄ production is shown.
env.energy_loss_note: Loss of feed gross energy as CH₄
env.herd_note: Herd size is taken from the animal count in the "Economic Analysis" section (default 1).
env.method: Method: Enteric CH₄ estimate — Moraes et al. (2014) primary, Niu et al. (2018) alternative. DMI (dry matter intake) is the primary determinant; NDF/forage increase it, dietary fat decreases it. Absolute values are calibrated to IPCC Tier 2 (~20 g CH₄/kg DMI) reference; intended for ration comparison and environmental impact indication (not individual animal prediction).

cncps.intro: CNCPS v6.5 detailed fermentation pools (ration level). Carbohydrate 8 pools (% DM), protein 6 pools (% CP). This profile shows which nutrient ferments at what rate in the rumen (acidosis/MFD/microbial synthesis management).
cncps.method: Calculation Mode (CNCPS iterative engine — dynamic AA + passage rate for calibrated feeds)

starch.msg_moderate: Ruminally fermented starch is in the typical range — balanced with adequate peNDF and buffering.

fq.msg_ok: All mycotoxins are below safe limits.
fq.msg_warning: One or more mycotoxins are approaching the limit — monitoring recommended.
fq.msg_danger: One or more mycotoxins exceed the limit — binder and/or feed change required.
fq.myco_footer: Limits are dairy total-ration (EU 2006/576/EC + FDA) risk indicators; aflatoxin transfers to milk as AFM1 (most critical). The rumen partially degrades DON/ZEN/OTA.
fq.silage_nodata: Silage fermentation analysis (pH, lactic/acetic/butyric acid, NH3-N) not entered. The quality score is computed once lab values are entered for silage feeds in the Feed Database.
fq.silage_footer: Score: pH (DM-adjusted), butyric acid (clostridial), NH₃-N (proteolysis), lactic dominance. Source: Kung & Shaver (2001).

inra.acc_title: 📊 Ration Average Unit Values (per kg DM)
inra.ufl_desc: Dairy feed energy unit (1 UFL ≈ 1700 kcal NEL)
inra.intro: INRA 2018 display layer — formulation uses NASEM 2021 + CNCPS v6.5 engine; INRA values are computed as supplemental report
inra.title: INRA 2018 Report

history.no_detail: No detail in this record (legacy entry).
history.confirm_del: Are you sure you want to delete this ration?
history.empty: No saved rations yet. Use the "💾 Save" button to add this ration to history.
history.count_info: {n} saved rations. Differences from the current ration are shown with 📈/📉.

onboard.s1_desc: On the Animal Profile tab, fill in body weight, milk yield, days in milk and other parameters. You can save the profile and assign it to herd groups.
onboard.s1_tip: BCS and lactation stage are critical for accurate DMI estimation.
onboard.s2_desc: Go to the Price Manager tab and click Load TR Reference Prices. Prices can be adjusted to the current market. Don't forget to save.
onboard.s2_tip: Correct prices → correct IOFC calculation → real economic analysis.
onboard.s3_desc: On the Feed Database tab, add the feeds for your ration with the + button. You can use the category filter and search.
onboard.s3_tip: Select at least 1 forage + 1 concentrate; the LP produces better results.
onboard.s4_desc: On the Ration Builder tab, set min/max amounts for each feed, then click Optimize Ration. The LP engine (GLPK) finds the lowest-cost feasible ration.
onboard.s4_tip: Entering a max limit like 2 kg/day for some feeds yields realistic results.
onboard.s5_desc: The Results tab shows diagnostics, amino acid balance, economic analysis and nutrient profile. Download a PDF or Excel report.
onboard.s5_tip: Green = on target; Yellow = low; Red = high — for quick assessment.
onboard.s6_desc: Using the Cloud infrastructure, you can create an account and sync your farm and data (profiles, feeds, rations, etc.) to the cloud. Click the cloud icon at the top to create your account and work seamlessly across multiple devices.
onboard.s6_tip: Don't forget to click the Sync button at the end of the day to avoid losing your work.

herd.add_profile_first: First add one or more profiles from the Animal Profile tab.
herd.info: All saved profiles are optimized with the same feed set. It uses the feed selection and constraints from the current Ration Builder tab.
herd.feed_hint: Selected on the Ration Builder tab
herd.select_feeds_first: First select feeds on the Ration Builder tab.
herd.hw_info: When groups are not independent but share a COMMON feed stock (e.g. limited corn silage on hand), this solves all groups together under the stock limit, minimizing total herd cost. With no stock entered the result equals batch (independent) optimization.
herd.hw_stock_hint: Optional: total daily feed stock (kg, as-fed). Empty = no limit. Only feeds you cap are constrained.
herd.hw_infeasible_hint: The shared stock may be insufficient to meet nutrient requirements — relax stock limits or add more feeds.
herd.hw_no_stock_set: No shared stock limit entered → result equals independent (batch) optimization.
herd.hw_budget_hint: Empty = unlimited. Herd-wide total daily feed cost ceiling.
herd.hw_micros_hint: Premix required; unmet micro-nutrients are reported (toxicity upper bound preserved).
herd.hw_note: Herd-wide v1: pure-LP total-cost minimization under shared feed stock + optional daily budget (NRC single-pass). Trace minerals/vitamins are optional-soft (premix). MILP and CNCPS calc mode are not yet supported in this mode.
herd.title: Farm Panel

imp.meta: Bulk feed entry — drag and drop or select your CSV or Excel (.xlsx) file.
imp.how_desc: The first row must be the header (e.g. name, category, dm, nel, cp). Turkish headers (Yem Adı, Kategori, KM, NEL, Ham Protein…) are also recognized. Required fields: Feed Name, Category, DM (%). Added feeds are marked as user feeds (editable/deletable).
imp.drop_here: Drop the file here or click to select
imp.template_done: Template downloaded. Fill it in and upload again.
imp.no_rows: No data rows found in the file. First row must be header, following rows data.
imp.warn_rows: ℹ️ Rows with warnings ({n}) — added anyway
imp.title: Import Feed

sens.binding_hint: Shadow price = marginal effect on daily cost of relaxing this constraint by 1 unit. Higher value = the constraint that drives cost most.
sens.no_binding: No binding constraints (solution is away from constraint bounds).
sens.entry_hint: These feeds are not cost-effective now; if their price drops by the shown amount (to the target price) they enter the ration.
sens.no_entry: No unused candidate feeds, or entry threshold could not be computed.
sens.whatif_hint: Use the slider to drop the feed's price and see live whether it enters the ration.
sens.note: ⚠️ Sensitivity is valid only with the 'cost only' objective + NO integer/min-order constraints + an optimal (non-relaxed) solution. Hidden in multi-objective or MILP mode.
sens.intro: LP shadow prices — shows constraint values and feed entry thresholds
sens.title: Sensitivity / Shadow Price

scen.btn_title: Compare different optimization settings (objective / calc mode) side by side for the same animal + feeds
scen.no_base: Optimize a ration first (scenarios build on the last optimization).
scen.intro: Compare different optimization strategies for the same profile
scen.title: Scenario Comparison

obs.typical: Typical measurement frequency: weekly milk yield, BCS every 2 weeks, monthly DMI weighing.
obs.csv_import_title: Upload CSV or JSON from milk analyzer (MilkoScan/Bentley)
obs.notes_ph: e.g. heat stress started, water cooling system activated
obs.clear_obs: 🗑 Delete All Observations for This Profile
obs.csv_no_data: No valid data found in the CSV file.
obs.empty_obs_hint: Add the first observation from the form above.
obs.trend_chart: 📈 Trend Chart (Milk / DMI / BCS — Time Series)
obs.trend_need2: At least 2 observations needed for the trend chart (current: {n}).
obs.perf_footer: Performance score: Calculated from predicted-vs-actual differences + trend direction. High score = ration predictions confirmed in the field.
obs.validation_intro: How close the ration prediction is to the measurements actually gathered in the field — formal accuracy metrics (RMSE / bias).
obs.val_bias_over: Positive bias → the model tends to OVER-predict DMI vs actual.
obs.val_bias_under: Negative bias → the model tends to UNDER-predict DMI vs actual.
obs.val_bias_neutral: Bias ~0 → DMI prediction shows no systematic direction (balanced).
obs.validation_insufficient: Reliable validation needs at least {need} DMI observations (currently {have}). Metrics become meaningful as more real DMI measurements are entered.
obs.validation_note: ⚠️ This is a validation FRAMEWORK, not model certification. With few observations from a single animal, metrics are only indicative; robust validation needs many animals/farms + real data over time (R² requires varying predictions).
obs.herd_val_intro: Multiple profiles produce varying DMI predictions → R² becomes meaningful. For each profile, the prediction (calcDMI) is compared to the mean of its observations.
obs.herd_val_insufficient: Multi-profile R² needs at least {need} profiles with DMI observations (currently {have}). Becomes meaningful as more profiles/observations are added.
obs.confirm_del: Are you sure you want to delete this observation?
obs.info: Field observations compare ration model to real data
obs.title: Field Observations

fe.cncps_measured_hint: This feed has measured CNCPS protein sub-fractions (soluble/NDF-insoluble/ADF-insoluble CP) → the CNCPS analysis panel is based on real data.
fe.cncps_derived_hint: No measured CNCPS protein sub-fractions → the CNCPS analysis panel uses category-derived defaults (approximate). The formulation engine always relies on book RDP/RUP.
fe.price_note: — use the "Price Manager" tab to edit
fe.g7: 🥛 Fatty Acid Profile (% total FA, optional)
fe.g12: 🌾 Silage Fermentation Quality (lab analysis, silage only)
fe.confirm_del: Are you sure you want to delete this feed?

pm.pending: ⚠️ You have unsaved changes. Click the Save button.
pm.snap_confirm: You have unsaved changes. Save first, then take a snapshot. Continue now?
pm.hist_empty_hint: Use the 📸 Snapshot button to save the current prices.
pm.records_note: Records are stored in browser IndexedDB. Total {n} snapshots.
pm.preset_applied: TR reference prices loaded for {n} feeds{region}. Don't forget to save.
pm.all_reset: All prices reset. Don't forget to save.
pm.intro: Enter feed prices and track history
pm.title: Price Manager

acalc.weather_no_loc: Please set your farm location on the Settings page first.
acalc.profile_new: New profile — click Save to add it.
acalc.confirm_del_profile: Are you sure you want to delete this profile?
acalc.confirm_del_group: Are you sure you want to delete this group?
acalc.preg_dry_title: Dry period applies only to pregnant cows — auto-checked
acalc.stage_closeup: ⚠ Close-up: milk fields locked · pregnancy month 9 · anionic DCAD target
acalc.stage_faroff: ⚠ Far-off: milk fields locked · pregnancy month 7-8 · low-energy ration
acalc.stage_lact: Stage selection auto-adjusts DCAD and CP targets
acalc.dmi_fill_hint: ℹ️ Shown DMI is animal-based; high-NDF rations may reduce intake during optimization (fill — shown in Results).
acalc.bgroup_note: B-group (B6/B2/B12/folic): rumen synthesis is usually sufficient; consider RP-folic+B12 in transition/high yield (Girard & Matte)

cloud.not_configured: Cloud is not configured. (No Supabase credentials in .env.) The app works fully offline without sign-in.
cloud.cloud_intro: Sign in to sync your data (profiles, rations, observations, farms) across all your devices. Sign-in is optional.
cloud.password_min: Password must be at least 6 characters.
cloud.reset_sent: Password reset link sent to your email.
cloud.signup_confirm: Account created. Click the confirmation link in your email, then sign in.
cloud.account_hint: Your data is stored in the cloud under this account and synced automatically across your devices.
cloud.logout_done: Signed out. Your data stays local on this device.
cloud.delete_account_confirm: Are you sure you want to delete your account? This action may cause loss of cloud data and cannot be undone.
cloud.err_already: This email is already registered. Try signing in.
cloud.settings_hint: Sign in with an account to sync your data across devices. Optional.
cloud.not_logged_in: Not signed in (local to this device only)

farm.hint: Manage multiple farms. Click a farm to switch to it — all data (profiles, rations, observations) is shown for the selected farm.
farm.delete_confirm: Farm "{name}" AND all its data (profiles, rations, observations) will be deleted. Are you sure?
farm.science_hint: Requirement system for this farm. If empty, the global system from Settings is used.
farm.title: Farm Management
